content: By 2025, the recursive self-improving memory architecture of cutting-edge autonomous self-supervised learning agents can be reframed as a layered biological ecosystem of nested sub-agents, where each sub-agent evolves its own intrinsic curiosity drive to explore unlabeled environmental niches instead of being explicitly assigned exploration goals. This nested curiosity ecosystem allows the full agent to continuously self-supervise learning by redistributing memory resources to the most surprising, high-growth sub-agent niches, outperforming 2024’s single-structure curiosity-driven active memory architectures.
